langs.name: Sindarin

langs.name: Avarin

langs.name: Valarin
langs.scope: ~30 long forms in Q&E; 'unfitting to Elvish organs'; loans into Quenya & Adûnaic (mirub→miruvórë, iniðil→inzil)

langs.name: Khuzdul
langs.scope: battle-cry + tomb + place-names + radical structure (kh-z-d); the grammar was never given

langs.name: Iglishmêk

langs.name: Adûnaic

langs.name: Westron

langs.name: Rohirric
langs.scope: genuine: kûd-dûkan, trahan, lô-; represented by Old English (App F device)

gimb- find. Tolkien gives the whole-line translation only; the morpheme division (durb- 'rule', -at, -ul, -ûk) is modern analysis, NOT his [I-M].
thrak- bring. Tolkien gives the whole-line translation only; the morpheme division (durb- 'rule', -at, -ul, -ûk) is modern analysis, NOT his [I-M].
krimp- bind. Tolkien gives the whole-line translation only; the morpheme division (durb- 'rule', -at, -ul, -ûk) is modern analysis, NOT his [I-M].

texts.cite: LotR II.8; Tolkien's prose rendering follows the poem in the text
texts.note: Glosses = Tolkien's own prose rendering, redistributed word-by-word [morpheme-splits I]
w: Ai! laurië lantar lassi súrinen
Ah! like gold fall the leaves in the wind
yéni únótimë ve rámar aldaron!
long years numberless as the wings of trees!
Yéni ve lintë yuldar avánier
The long years have passed like swift draughts
mi oromardi lissë-miruvóreva
of the sweet mead in lofty halls
Andúnë pella, Vardo tellumar nu luini
beyond the West, beneath the blue vaults of Varda
yassen tintilar i eleni ómaryo airetári-lírinen.
wherein the stars tremble in the song of her voice, holy and queenly.
Sí man i yulma nin enquantuva?
Who now shall refill the cup for me?
An sí Tintallë Varda Oiolossëo ve fanyar máryat Elentári ortanë
For now the Kindler, Varda, the Queen of the Stars, from Mount Everwhite has uplifted her hands like clouds
ar ilyë tier undulávë lumbulë
and all paths are drowned deep in shadow
ar sindanóriello caita mornië i falmalinnar imbë met,
and out of a grey country darkness lies on the foaming waves between us,
ar hísië untúpa Calaciryo míri oialë.
and mist covers the jewels of Calacirya for ever.
Sí vanwa ná, Rómello vanwa, Valimar!
Now lost, lost to those from the East, is Valimar!
Namárië! Nai hiruvalyë Valimar!
Farewell! Maybe thou shalt find Valimar!
Nai elyë hiruva! Namárië!
Maybe even thou shalt find it! Farewell!

elements: (plural Edain) in Adanedhel, Aradan, Dúnedain. For its meaning and history see Atani in the Index.
‘glory, brilliance’ in Dagor Aglareb, Aglarond. The form in Quenya, alkar, has transposition of the consonants: to Sindarin aglareb corresponds Alkarinquë. The root is kal-‘shine’, q.v.
‘tree’ (Quenya) in Aldaron, Aldudenië, Malinalda, corresponding to Sindarin galadh (seen in Caras Galadhon and the Galadhrim of Lothlórien).
‘swan’ (Sindarin alph) in Alqualondë; from a root alak-‘rushing’ occurring also in Ancalagon.
‘doom’ in Amon Amarth, Cabed Naeramarth,Úmarth, and in the Sindarin form of Túrin’s name ‘Master of Doom’, Turamarth. The Quenya form of the word appears in Turambar.
‘hill’, a Sindarin word occurring as the first element of many names; plural emyn in Emyn Beraid.
‘jaws’ in Ancalagon (for the second element in this name see alqua).
‘long’ in Andram, Anduin; also in Anfalas (‘Langstrand’) in Gondor, Cair Andros (‘ship of long-foam’) an island in Anduin, and Angerthas ‘long rune-rows’.
‘sunset, west’ in Andúnië, to which corresponds in Sindarin annûn, cf. Annúminas, and Henneth Annûn ‘window of the sunset’ in Ithilien. The
‘iron’, Sindarin ang, in Angainor, Angband, Anghabar, Anglachel, Angrist, Angrod, Anguirel, Gurthang; angren ‘of iron’ in Angren…
‘gift’ in Annatar, Melian, Yavanna; the same stem in Andor ‘Land of Gift’.
‘great door or gate’, plural ennyn, in Annon-in-Gelydh; cf. Morannon the ‘Black Gate’ of Mordor and Sirannon the ‘Gate-stream’ of Moria.

‘beside, outside’ (whence Quenya ar ‘and’, Sindarin a), probably in Araman ‘outside Aman’; cf. also (Nirnaeth) Arnoediad ‘(Tears) without reckoning’. ar(a)‘high, noble, royal’ appears in a great many names, as Aradan, …
‘father’ in Atanatári (see Atani in Index), Ilúvatar.
‘prison, duress’ in Angband; from original mbando, of which the Quenya form appears in Mandos (Sindarin Ang-band = Quenya Angamando).

‘dwelling’ in Bar-en-Danwedh. The ancient word mbár (Quenya már, Sindarin bar) meant the ‘home’ both of persons and of peoples, and thus appears in many place-names, as Brithombar, Dimbar (the first element of which means ‘sad, gloomy’), Eldamar, Val(i)mar, Vinyamar, MarnuFalmar. Mardil, name of the first of the Ruling Stewards of Gondor, means ‘devoted t…
‘tower’ in Barad-dûr, Barad Eithel, Barad Nimras; the plural in Emyn Beraid.
‘mighty’ in Beleg, Belegaer, Belegost, Laer Cú Beleg.
‘gravel’ in Brithiach, Brithombar, Brithon. (For many names beginning with C see entries under K)
(galen) the usual Sindarin word for ‘green’, in Ard-galen, Tol Galen, Calenardhon; also in Parth Galen (‘Green Sward’) beside Anduin and Pinnath Gelin (‘Green Ridges’) in Gondor. See kal-.

(from kambā) ‘hand’, but specifically of the hand held cupped in the attitude of receiving or holding, in Camlost, Erchamion. carak- This root is seen in Quenya carca ‘fang’, of which the Sindarin form

occurs in Carcharoth, and also in Carchost (‘Fang Fort’, one of the Towers of the Teeth at the entrance to Mordor). Cf. Caragdûr, Carach Angren (‘Iron Jaws’, the rampart and dike guarding the entrance to Udûn in Mordor), and Helcaraxë.

‘red’, Quenya carnë, in Caranthir, Carnil, Orocarni; also in Caradhras, from caran-rass, the ‘Red-horn’ in the Misty Mountains, and Carnimírië ‘red-jewelled’, the rowan-tree in Quickbeam’s song. The translation of Carcharoth in the text as ‘Red Maw’ must depend on association with this word; see carak-.

‘silver’ (Quenya telep, telpë, as in Telperion) in Celeborn, Celebrant, Celebros. Celebrimbor means ‘silver-fist’, from the adjective celebrin ‘silver’ (meaning not ‘made of silver’ but ‘like silver, in hue or worth’) and
(Quenya quárë) ‘fist’, often used to mean ‘hand’; the Quenya form of the name was Telperinquar. Celebrindal has celebrin and tal, dal ‘foot’.
‘mound’ in Corollairë (also called Coron Oiolairë, which latter word
‘bow’ in Cúthalion, Dor Cúarthol, Laer Cú Beleg.

‘awakening’ in Cuiviénen (Sindarin Nen Echui). Other derivatives of the same root are Dor Firn-i-Guinar; coirë, the first beginning of Spring, Sindarin echuir, The Lord of the Rings Appendix D; and coimas ‘life-bread’, Quenya name of lembas.
‘shadow’ in Dor Daedeloth, and perhaps in Daeron.
‘battle’; the root is ndak-, cf. Haudh-en-Ndengin. Another derivative is Dagnir (Dagnir Glaurunga ‘Glaurung’s Bane’).
‘horror’ in Deldúwath; deloth ‘abhorrence’ in Dor Daedeloth.
‘silent’ in Dor Dínen; cf. Rath Dínen, the Silent Street in Minas Tirith, and Amon Dîn, one of the beacon-hills of Gondor.
‘head’ in Lórindol; often applied to hills and mountains, as in Dol Guldur, Dolmed, Mindolluin (also Nardol, one of the beacon-hills of Gondor, and Fanuidhol, one of the Mountains of Moria).

‘land’ (i.e. dry land as opposed to sea) was derived from ndor; it occurs in many Sindarin names, as Doriath, Dorthonion, Eriador, Gondor, Mordor, etc. In Quenya the stem was blended and confused with a quite distinct

nórë meaning ‘people’; in origin Valinórë was strictly ‘the people of the Valar’, but Valandor ‘the land of the Valar’, and similarly Númen(n)órë ‘people of the West’, but Númendor ‘land of the West’. Quenya Endor ‘Middle-earth’ was from ened ‘middle’ and ndor; this in Sindarin became Ennor (cf.
‘middle lands’ in the chant A Elbereth Gilthoniel).

Quenya lómë; thus Sindarin dúlin ‘nightingale’ corresponds to lómelindë.

‘sea’ (Quenya) in Eärendil, Eärrámë, and many other names. The Sindarin word gaer (in Belegaer) is apparently derived from the same original stem.

‘one, alone’, in Amon Ereb (cf. Erebor, the Lonely Mountain), Erchamion, Eressëa, Eru.

‘shuddering’ in Nen Girith; cf. also Girithron, name of the last month of the year in Sindarin (The Lord of the Rings Appendix D).

Q kopa harbour, bay. [This entry was struck out; see KHOP.]

round. *korna : Q korna round, globed; koron (kornen) globe, ball; koromindo cupola, dome. Kor round hill upon which Tuna (Tun) was built. N corn, coron, Cor (koro). [Cor > Caury and the following added:] [Q] korin circular enclosure [cf. I. 257]; N cerin. N rhin-gorn circle [RIN]. Cf. Ilk. basgorn [se. bast-gorn 'round bread', loaf: MBAS

strive, quarrel. mokta strife: Qohta war. N auth War, battle;cost quarrel (kot-t-)t Q kosta- quarrel. [The base was changed to KOTH, and the following added:] Q kotumo enemy, kotya hostile. [N] coth enmity, enemy; cf. Morgoth - but this may also contain GOTH. [See OKTA.]
press. N cramb, cram cake of compressed flour or meal (often containing honey and milk) used on long journey. [Added entry.]
*kukuwa dove; Q ku, kuay ON ku, kua, (= kuua)\ N cugu. [Added entry. The base-stem is not given but is taken from a later etymological note.]
Qkumbe mound, heap; N cumb, cum. [Added entry.]
bow. > kuw: Q ku bow; N cu arch, crescent; curan the crescent moon, see RAN. [Added:] *ku3na: N cun bowed, bowshaped, bent; but Ilk. *kogna >coun> caun, Dan. cogn.

gold (metal). Q kulu, N col; Q kuluinn of gold. KULU- gold (substance). Q kulo. [This entry was struck out and the following roughly substituted:] KUL- golden-red. Q f kullo red gold; kulda, kulina flame-coloured, golden-red; kuluina orange; kuluma an orange; N coll red (*kulda).

void. Q kuma the Void; kumna empty; N cun empty. [The Q forms were retained, but the Noldorin altered to read:] ON kuma, N cofn, caun empty, void, but in EN [the Void was] called Gast, Belegast [cf. GAS] .
prince. Q kundu ; N cunn, especially in names as Felagund, Baragund. [Added entry.]
craft. Q kuruoe craft. N curie, curu ; curunir wizard; cf. Curufin [PHIN]. Cf. N crum wile, guile; corw cunning, wily. [Added entry. N crum was rejected; see KURUM.]
N crum the left hand; crom left; crumui left-handed (*krumbe, -a). [Added entry. Cf. KHYAR.]

come to life, awake. Q kuile life, being alive; kuina alive; kuive (noun) awakening; kuivea (adj.) wakening; kuivie = kuive, cf. Kuivienen. N cuil life; cuin alive; echui(w) awakening (*et~kuiwe), hence Sen-Echui = Q Kuivienen. [The following additions were made:] N cuino to be alive; Dor Firn iguinar Land of the Dead that Live.
die in pain. Q qalme agony, death; qalin dead; unqale agony, death. [Added entry. See WAN.]
Q qame sickness; N paw; Ilk. corn. [Added entry.]

clutching hand, fist. Q qar hand (qari)\ N paur fist. [This stem was not struck out, but a second form of it was put in elsewhere in the list:] KWAR- Q qare fist; ON pore\ N paur, -bor, cf. Celebrimbor Silver-fist.
Q qanta full; ON panta; N pant full, cf. Cambant [KAB]; pathred fullness\pannod ovpathro fill. [Added entry.]

fade, wither. Cf. Sarqelion fire-fading, autumn, N lhasbelin [LAS 1 ]. *kwelett- corpse: Qqelet, qeletsi. KWEN(ED)- Elf. *kvcenede\ Q qende Elf; N penedh, pi. penidh; Dan. cwenda. Q Qendelie, N Penedhrim. T h e word Eledh is usually employed. [Added entry.]
*kivesse : Q qesse feather; Ilk. cwess down; N pesseg pillow (Qqesset). [Added entry.]

(and PET-) say. *kivetta: N peth word. *kwenta tale: N pent, Q qenta; N pennas history. *kwentrv narrator: Q qentaro\ N pethron\ Dor. civindor. [Added:] Q qetil tongue, language; qentale account, history; lumeqentale history [LU]. N gobennas history, gobennathren historical. Q avaqet- refuse, forbid [AWA]. [For goprefixsee wo.]
Cf. KU3 *kzvinga: Q qinga bow (for shooting); N peng. [Added entry.]
come to an end. Q tyel- end, cease; tyel (tyelde) end; tyelima final. Cf. TELES. [Added entry.]
swift, agile. Q tyelka; N celeg, cf. Celegorn [GOR] .

and TELEP- silver. N celeb silver; Q telpe and tyelpe silver; telepsa of silver = telpina, N celebren. Cf. Irilde Taltelepsa = THE ETYMOLOGIES Idhril Gelebrendal. [celebren> Gelebrendal early changed from celebrin, CelebrindaL The entry was rewritten thus:] KYELEP- (and TELEP?) silver. ON kelepe, N celeb, silver; Q telpe and tyelpe silver

no, not. Q la and lala, also lau> laume (= la ume [UGU]), no, no indeed not, on the contrary; also used for asking incredulous questions. As prefix la- > [vocalic] / > Q il9 N a/, as in Ilkorin, N Alchoron, pi. Elcheryn. Q lala- to deny. [See AR2.]
lick. Q lamba tongue, N Iham(b). Q lavin I lick, also lapsa to lick (frequentative). N Ihefi (Ihaf).
Cf. DAL, LAT. Q landa wide, N lhand, lhann. N camland palm of hand. Cf. Lhothland, Lhothlann (empty and wide), name of a region [LUS] .
Q lango broad sword; also prow of a ship. N lhang cutlass, sword.

keen, sharp, acute. Q lathe > N lhaeg. Q laike acuteness, keenness of perception. Ilk. laig keen, sharp, fresh, lively (blended with laikwa [see LAYAK]). LAK1- swallow; cf. LANK. Q lanko throat. LAK2- swift (cf. ALAK). mlakra: Q larka swift, rapid, also alarka; N lhagr, Ihegin.
elm-tree. Q alalme\ N lhalwen (lelwin)y lhalorn\ D lalrn. [See ALAM.]

Q lamya to sound; lama ringing sound, echo; lamma a sound; lamina echoing; nallama echo. Dor. lom echo, lomen echoing. Thus Dor. Lomendor, Lominorthin, Noldorinized > Dorlomen, Ered Lomin; pure N Eredlemrin, Dorlamren. See GLAM.
weave. Q lanya weave; lanwa loom; lanat weft; lanne tissue, cloth.
Q lanko throat; N lhanc. [This stem was first written LANG, with derivatives Q lango (*langwi), N lhang. See LAK1.]

Q lapse babe; N lhaes. LAS1- *lasse leaf: Q lasse, N lhass; Qlasselanta leaf-fall, autumn, N lhasbelin (mlassekwelene), cf. Q Narqelion [KWEL]. Lhasgalen Greenleaf, Gnome name of Laurelin. (Some think this is related to the next and *lasse 'ear*. T h e Quendian ears were more pointed and leafshaped than [?human].) LAS 2 - listen. N lhaw e
string, thong. Q laita strap; N lhath thong of [?leather]; cf. hadlath, haglath sling (KHAT).

warm. *lauka warm: Q lauka, N lhaug. LAWAR-, N GLAWAR- Vaure (light of the golden Tree Laurelin) gold - the metal was properly smalta, see SMAL; Q laure, N glaur, Dor. Oss. laur. Hence N glor~, lor- in names, as Glorfindel [SPIN], Inglor [ I D ] . Cf. Laurelin, N Galad-loriel\ Rathloriel [RAT]. Nglawar sunlight, radiance of Laurelin; f Gl

*laik ~wa: Q laiqa green; N Ihoeb fresh - 'green' only in Q Laiqendi Green-elves, N Lhoebenidh or Lhoebelidh. Ilk. laig is blended with laika [LAIK] . LEB-, LEM- stay, stick, adhere, remain, tarry. Q lemba (*lebna) left behind, pi. Lembi Elves remaining behind = Telerin Ilkorins; N Ihevon, Ihifnir. [See p. 344.] LEDgo, fare, travel. Cf. Q

loose, let loose, release. N Ihein, lhain free(d); Iheitho to release, set free; Iheithian release, freeing. Q leuka, lehta loose, slacken. Ilk. legol nimble, active, running free; cf. Legolin, a river-name. [A note on a slip accompanying these etymologies gives: 'Leth- set free (cf. LED); EN leithia to release, leithian release; cf. Lay

many. Q lie people; -// pi. suffix, lin- prefix=many, aslintyulussea having many poplars [TYUL], lindornea having many oaks [DORON]. In N the ending -lin 'many' has been blended with rhitn > Hm, rim. LIB '- drip. Q limba a drop; cf. helkelimbe [KHELEK] . LIB 2 - *laibe: Q laive ointment. N shows GLIB-: glaew salve. *libda: Q lipsa; N [Ihu

dance. Q lilta- dance. LIN 1 - pool. Q liny a pool; N/Am; Ilk. line. Ci.Ailin [AY], Taiglin. LIN 2 - (originally GLIN) sing. Q linde air, tune; N Ihind, Mnn. Q Undo singer, singing bird: cf. tuilindo swallow, N tuilinn [TUY], Q lomelinde nightingale, N dulinn. Q lindele music. Cf. Laurelin (g.sg. Laurelinden), but this also taken as 'hang

fair (especially of voice); in Q blended with slinda (see SLIN). Q linda fair, beautiful, cf. Undar\ N Ihend tuneful, sweet; Ilk. lind. LING-, N GLING- hang. Q linga- hang, dangle; Ngling. Cf. Glingal [and see L I N 2 ] .

Q linqe wet. N lhimp\ Ihimmid moisten (pa.t. Ihimmint). LIPQ limpe (wine), drink of the Valar. [The first appearance of limpe since the Lost Tales, where it was the drink of the Elves; for the old etymology see I. 258.] LIR1- sing, trill; in Ng-lir- [see GLIR]. Q lirin I chant. LIR2- ON lire row, range, N Mr row. Cf. oeglir range of mount
*linwi fish: Q lingwe; N Ihimb, Mm; Dor. liw.
*londe narrow path, strait, pass: N Ihonn (cf. Aglon)\ cf. N othlondy othlon paved way (ost city + lond). Q londe road (in sea), entrance to harbour, cf. Alqalonde.

great serpent, dragon. Q loke (-/') dragon; anguloke dragon [ANGWA], ramaloke winged dragon [RAM], uruloke fire-dragon [UR], fealoke spark-dragon [PHAY], lingwiloke fish-dragon, sea-serpent [LIW]. Cf. N Ihug, amlug, Ihimlug.
Q lokse hair; N thaws, Ihoch (*lokko) ringlet.

lona island, remote land difficult to reach. Cf. Avalona [AWA] = Tol Eressea = the outer isle. [Added to this is A-val-lon. Avallon first appears in the second version of The Fall ofNumenor (§ i) as a name of Tol Eressea with the explanation that 'it is hard by Valinor'.]

sleep. Q olor dream, cf. Ijorien = N Lhuien. Q lore slumber, lorna asleep. N ol dream, oltha [to dream]. [See OLOS.] LOT(H) flower. Q lote (large single) flower; losse blossom (usually, owing to association with olosse snow, only used of white blossom [see GOLOS]). N Ihoth flower\gwaloth blossom, collection of flowers [wo]. Cf. Wingelot,

Q lume time (cf. lumeqenta history, chronological account, lumeqentale history, lumeqentalea historical); Iu a time, occasion. N Ihu. [SeeKWET.] LUG ! - *lunga heavy: Q lunga ; N Ihong; Dor. lung; cf. Dor. Mablung [MAP] . LUG2- *lugni blue: Q lune; N Ihun (Dor. luin pale, Dan. lygn). Cf. Lunoronti Blue Mountains, N Eredluin (also Lhunorod

magic, enchantment. N Ihuth spell, charm; Ihutha to enchant; Lhuthien enchantress (Dor. Luithien). Q luke enchantment; luhta enchant. [The etymology of Luthien changed to read thus:] Doriath luthy whence Luthien (Noldorized as Lhuthien): *luktiene.
Q lumbe gloom, shadow; Hisilumbe, N Hithlum [KHIS]. In Q the form is usually Hisilome by attraction of lome night [DO3]. N Ihum shade, Ihumren shady.
N Ihost empty, cf. [Mablothren >] Camlost [KAB], Lothlann [LAD]. Qlusta void, empty.

float, swim. Q lunte boat; N Ihunt. N Ihoda float. M [The M-entries are faint and difficult to interpret, and some are very confused. My father made a beginning on a new list, writing the THE ETYMOLOGIES etymologies out afresh and clearly, but this petered out after he had treated the stems in MA- and a few others (MBAD, MBER, MEL).]
Q maty a pale, fallow, fawn. N meiO, maio, hence Maidhros (anglicized Maidros) = 'pale-glitter' [RUS].

hand. PQ *ma3 (waj-) hand: Q ma\ ON mo (pi. mat) usually replaced by kamba (N camm): see KAB. Hence *ma3tti handy, skilled, Q matte (pi. maisi)', ON maite, N moed. *mayta to handle: Eld. *mahta-\ Q mahta-, ON mattho-be, N matho stroke, feel, handle; wield (confused with *makta, see MAK). Related is MAG- use, handle, in *magra useful, fit,

sword, or as verb-stem: fight (with sword), cleave. *makla : Q makil sword; N magi, magol. *makta: Q mahta- wield a weapon (blended with ma^-ta, see MA3), fight: hence mahtar warrior = N maethor. N maeth battle, fight (not of general host but of two or a few), maetha to fight. Cf. Maglaour [cf. D03?] or Maglaohonn = Black-sword (as name).
holy spirit (one who has not been born or who has passed through death). Q manu departed spirit; N man. Cf. Q Manwe (also borrowed and used in N [see WEG]).
doom, final end, fate, fortune (usually = final bliss). Q manar, mande. N manaO. Cf. N manathon. In Q this stem is partly blended with MBAD, q.v. and cf. Mandos, Kalamando.

lay hold of with hand, seize. Q mapa- grasp, seize. ON mapseize, take away by force. Ilk. (Dor.) mab hand (*mapa), cf. Mablung [LUG 1 ]. Ilk. Ermab(r)in one-handed (of Beren: cf. Mablosgen emptyhanded = N Erchamron, Camlost). [The forms Ermab(r)in and Erchamron are certain.]
knead, make soft by rubbing, kneading, etc. *mazga: Q maksa pliant, soft; ON mazga > maiga, N moe, soft. *mazge: Q makse dough, N moeas dough. Ilk. maig dough.
eat. Q mat-; N medi. For megli bear see LIS.

duress, prison, doom, hell. *mbanda: N band, bann duress, prison; Angband Hell (Iron-prison) (Q Angamanda). Q Mando the Imprisoner or Binder, usually lengthened Mand-os (Mandosse = Dread Imprisoner, N. Bannos [cos]). Blended with Q with MAN - hence Kalamando Light Mando = Manwe, Morimando Dark Mando = Mandos. MBAD is in turn related to BA
exchange. Q manka- trade; makar tradesman; mankale commerce. N banc, banga\ bachor pedlar; bach article (for exchange), ware, thing (*mbakha).
Q malle street; ambal shaped stone, flag.

dwell, inhabit. Q a-mbar (ambaron) 'oikoumene', Earth; Endamar, Ambarenya Middle-earth. N ambar, amar Earth; Emmeretriy Emerin (Ambarenya) Middle-earth. Martan(o) Earthbuilder = Aule (N Barthan) [TAN]. Gondobar, Findobar [PHIN]. [With the use of the Greek word oikoumene here cf. Letters no. 154, p. 197. Ambarendya occurs in the Ambarkanta
Q umbar (umbarten) fate, doom; N ammarth. Q marta fey, fated; maranwe destiny; martya- destine. N barad doomed; bartho to doom. Cf. Turamarth, Q Turambar [apparently written thus over Turumbar].
knead. Q masta- bake, masta bread. N bast bread; basgorn loaf [KOR] .

compel, force, subject, oppress. Q mauya- compel; mausta compulsion; maure need. N baug tyrannous, cruel, oppressive; bauglo to oppress; bauglir tyrant, oppressor; bui (*mauy-) (impersonal) \baurneed. Cf. Gothmog (*Gothombauk-) [cos].

Q meren (merend-) or merende feast, festival; N bereth. Q merya festive; meryale holiday. N beren festive, gay, joyous. [This stem was first MER, and the N words mereth, meren; but a new stem MER was then introduced and the former MER changed to MBER, the N words becoming bereth, beren. The name Mereth Aderthad was never changed in the te
(compound of MIR and RIL, q.v.) Q miril (mirilli) shining jewel; mirilya- glitter. Ilk. bril glass, crystal; cf. Brilthor glittering torrent.
Dor. moth pool, umboth large pool. Cf. Q motto blot, N both puddle, small pool. Cf. Umboth Muilin [MUY] = N Elinuial or Hithltniath.
project. *mbundu: Q mundo snout, nose, cape; N bund, bunn. Cf. *andambunda long-snouted, Q andamunda elephant, N andabon, annabon [ANAD].

love (as friend). Q mel-\ melin dear, melda beloved, dear; melme love; melisse (f.), melindo (m.) lover; melima loveable, fair, Melimar = Lindar. Irregular vocalism: mmalo friend, Q malo, N meleth love; mell dear; mellon friend; meldir friend, f. meldis; melethron, melethril lover, mil love, affection; milui friendly, loving, kind.
Qmen place, spot; mena region. Cf. Nurnen, Romen, Harmen THE ETYMOLOGIES [see KHYAR], Tormen [which is the form in the Ambarkanta, IV. 244-5, 24^~9» changed later to Formen (PHOR).]
wish, desire, want. Q mere, pa.t. merne. [See MBER.]

[This entry was struck out, and the stem MBEREK written against it. It was the same as the entry BEREK, q.v., except that the Q form was here merka 'wild' for verka, a N form brerg 'wild,fierce*was given, and bregol was translated 'fierce'.]
end. Q mente point, end; N ment point; meth end (*metta); methen end. Q metya- put an end to.

inside. Q mi in, within; mir and minna to the inside, into; mitya adj. interior. [The stem vowel ae in the N words was changed to oe: Moeleg, etc. T h e Gnomish name Moeleg o

stand alone, stick out. Q mine one; minya first; minda prominent, conspicuous; mindo isolated tower. N min one, minei (*miniia) single, distinct, unique; minnas tower, also mindon (*minitaun, cf. tunn [seeTUN]). MINIK-W- Q minqe eleven.

Q, ON mire; N mir jewel, precious thing, treasure. Cf. Nauglamtr (Doriathrin form). Mirion ordinary N name of the Silevril (Silmarilli), pi. Miruin; = N Golo(O)vir or Mir in GeleiO, Dor. Goldamir. [The nameBorommiro is scribbled in: see BOR.]

go free, stray, wander. Q mirima free; cf. Mirimor = the Teleri. mista- stray about. N mist error, wandering; misto to stray; mistrad straying, error. [In the long note to QS §29 giving names 'in song and tale' of the Kindreds of the Elves a name of the Teleri is 'the Free' (and another 'the Wanderers').]
Q miksa wet; N rnesg, me se.
N mith white fog, wet mist; cf. Mithrim [RINGI]. [Later addition: mith = grey.]
whine. Q maiwe gull, N maew. Q miule whining, mewing.

*mizde: Q miste fine rain; N midh dew; Dor. mid moisture (adj. med wet, *mizda); Dan. meord fine rain. Cf. Dor. nameDolmed 'Wet-head' [NDOL]. [The stems MISK-, MITH-, MIZD- are evidently related, but it is scarcely possible to see from the changes on the manuscript what my father finally intended.]
*mci-: Q mol slave, thrall; N mul. Q mota- labour, toil; N mudo (pa.t. mudas). [Cf. Lhammas §8: mulanoldorin > molanoldorin, language of the Noldor enslaved by Morgoth.]

*mori black: Q more black (N ^mor)\ mordo shadow, obscurity, stain; more blackness, dark, night; morna gloomy, sombre; morilinde nightingale (Ilk. murulind, myrilind). N maurgloom;moru black. Ilk. mor night. Meglivorn: see LIS, MAT. Morgoth Black Foe [KOT] = Melko. Morimando = Mandos [see MBAD]. Moriqendi Dark Elves = Morimor, N Duveledh
*moroko bear: Q morko; N brog; Ilk. broga. [See LIS.]
Q moina familiar, dear; ON muina, N muin dear. [See TOR.]

Q muina hidden, secret; muile secrecy. Dor. muilin secret, veiled; Umboth Muilin veiled pool = N Lhin Vial or Eilinuial. Dor. muil twilight, shadow, vagueness. (Not in N because it became identical with moina [MOY] .) N [There was no new start made on the N-entries, which remain in their extremely difficult original form. The stems with a
Q nanda water-mead, watered plain. N nand, nann wide grassland; naoor, nadhras pasture. Dor. nand field, valley. Cf. Nandungorthin, Nan Tathren.

[Cf. ANAK] bite. Q nak- bite; N nag-. Q nahta a bite; N naeth biting, gnashing of teeth [see NAY]. N naew (*nakma)y Q nangwa jaw. Cf. *an-ka jaw, row of teeth: Q anka, N anc\ Anc-alagon 'Biting-Storm', dragon-name [ALAK].

N nana (hypocoristic) mother; naneth. [See AM1.] NAR1- flame, fire. Q nar and nare flame, cf. Anar Sun; narwa fiery red. N naur flame; Anar Sun; narwy nam red. Cf. Egnor [EK], etc.; for Feanor see PHAY. Q narqelion 'fire-fading', autumn [KWEL]. [The N form Anar is clear. See ANAR.] NAR2- (Q nyar-) tell, relate. Q nyare tale, saga, history

tear, rend (tr. and intr.). *naraka rushing, rapid, violent: Q naraka harsh, rending, violent; N narcha- to rend, Q narki. NNarog river-name; Nar(o)gothrond [os] = fortress of Narog; Narogardh = realm of Narog.

point, sharp end. Q nasse thorn, spike; nasta- prick, sting. N THE ETYMOLOGIES nass point, sharp end; angle or corner (cf. BEN); nasta prick, point, stick, thrust. Cf. SNAS, SNAT. NAT(cf. NUT) lace, weave, tie. Q natse web, net; N nath web; Dor. nass. N nathron weaver, webster \ gonathra- entangle, enmesh, gonathras entanglement. [For pre

T h e true N idiom is mxr na Nauglin or Nauglvir > Nauglavir. [N naug was struck out and replaced by: 'N nawag (pi. neweig, neweg); Dor. naugol, whence EN naugl

lament, naeth (nakt-) 'biting* is associated in N with this stem, and gets senses of gnashing teeth in grief: cf. Nirnaeth Arnediad (or Aronoded) [NOT]. Q naire lament, naina- lament. (or perhaps NAYKA-, elaboration of NAK, q.v.) pain. Q naike sharp pain; naikele; naikelea painful. N naeg pain; negro to pain.

slay. ON ndakie to slay, pa.t. ndanke; ndagno slain (as noun), corpse; ndakro slaughter, battle. N degi to slay; daen corpse; dangen slain, cf. HauOiNdengin; dagrt dagor battle; dagro to battle, make war. *ndako warrior, soldier: ON ndoko, N daug chiefly used of Orcs, also called Boldog. [Boldog is an Ore-captain in the Lay of Leithian an
hammer, beat. Q namba a hammer, namba- to hammer. Nambarauto hammerer of copper, sixth son of Feanor, N Damrod [RAUTA]. N dam a hammer, damna- to hammer (pa.t. dammint).

back. (Cf. Danas; N Dan, pi. Dein, Dadhrin). Q nan(prefix) backwards. Dor. don back (noun). Cf. Q na, nan but, on the contrary, on the other hand, a-nanta and yet, but yet. [See DAN, and commentary on Uiammas §7.]

strengthened form of der man (see DER). *ndero bridegroom > Eldarin ndaer, Q ner man (blended with der)\ ON ndair y N doer bridegroom. Cf. Ender surname of Tulkas (Endero), as Indis (see NDIS) of his wife.
follow, come behind. Q neuna (*ndeuna) second; *ndeuro follower, successor: Q neuro, cf. Dor. Dior successor (i.e. of Thingol). The stem is confused with NDU 'sink' in N .

Strengthening (parallel to NDER of DER) of NIS 'woman', itself elaborated from INI. NDIS-SE/SA Q nisse beside nis (see NIS, NI) woman. ON ndissa young woman (in N dess was blended with bess, properly 'wife'); m ndise bride > ON ndis, N dis. Intensive form mi-ndise = Q Indis *bride\ name of the goddess Nessa. 37<>
Q nola round head, knoll; N dol (ON ndolo) head. Cf. Q Andolat hill-name, N Dolad. N dolt (pi. dylt) round knob, boss. Cf. Dor. Ndolmed, Dolmed = Wet Head, name of mountain in Eredlindon.

dwell, stay, rest, abide. Q nore land, dwelling-place, region where certain people live, as Vali-nore (Valinor). The long vowel in Q is due to confusion with nore clan (NO; ONO). N dor i*ndore); dortho- dwell, stay. Cf. Endor = Endamar Middle-earth. Doriath: see GATH. [Under ENED Endor is defined as 'centre of the world'. See IV. 254-5.]

(see also NU) go down, sink, set (of Sun, etc). Associated in N with D03 night, also with NDEW. Q nurnen west (see MEN), numenya western; nuta set, sink (of Sun or Moon); andune (*ndune) sunset.
